t: Epic — Station Master + Lines: Track Assembly & Continuity Layer

content.what: Build the Station Master handler and Lines data model. The Station Master is a headless logic layer (lib/station/) that sits between /api/tracks and the DB. It normalizes incoming WAI Track payloads (v0.18 HSP and WAI Claude formats), detects fork divergence, assigns sessions to Lines, and manages the merge ledger. Lines are topical continuity rails — a Line is a DB row that groups many Tracks. Wheel → Line → Track = Library → Book → Chapter.
content.why: Direct DB writes have no retry, no assembly, no topology awareness. The Station Master addresses all three and makes the Vault a proper persistent project memory, not just a flat artifact store.

design.station_home: lib/station/ — pure logic, no Next.js imports, extractable to Go/Rust later
design.fork_trigger: session_codename collision where Turn 1 user.raw diverges from stored Turn 1
design.merge_trigger: state_snapshot from fork promoted to main Line — deliberate action
design.line_self_assignment: Agents embed line_id in session_header; Station respects it or auto-assigns

_behavior_directive.what_this_is: An epic tracking the Station Master and Lines implementation — 8 child tasks, schema-first. TRACK this in the task graph.
_behavior_directive.what_this_is_NOT: An instruction to execute immediately or automatically. Do not start building without explicit session focus. Work child tasks in seq order.
_behavior_directive.processing_agent: wakeup briefing surfaces this as active epic; user selects child task to begin
_behavior_directive.expected_outcome: Station Master live in lib/station/, /api/tracks delegates to it, Lines CRUD available at /api/lines
